channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dom Guyot: Cebuano Artist Making Waves in Manila’s Music Scene
item.description: Dom Guyot, a multitalented singer, songwriter, and dancer from Cebu, is steadily carving a name for himself in the Philippine music scene. Growing up inspired by pop and R&B icons like Beyoncé, Rihann...

item.title: Victor Osimhen hails his happiest period at Galatasaray after helping them win fourth straight title
item.description: Victor Osimhen, di Super Eagles striker, don talk say him dey enjoy di most fulfilling part of him career since e join Galatasaray. Di Nigerian forward don really shine for Turkey dis season, scoring ...

item.title: Jen Psaki Presents Evidence Contradicting Eric Trump's Board Membership Denial
item.description: During her Friday night show 'The Briefing,' former White House press secretary Jen Psaki fact-checked Eric Trump's claims in real time after he threatened legal action against her and the network for...

item.title: Courts in Karachi postpone decision on police request for Anmol’s custody in multiple narcotics cases
item.description: On Saturday, courts in Karachi deferred their rulings on police applications seeking the physical remand of Anmol, also known as Pinky, who is alleged to be a major figure in the narcotics trade. The ...

item.title: Montreal sex workers plan strike to demand safer working conditions and labour protections
item.description: A collective of sex workers in Montreal, represented by the Sex Work Autonomous Committee, has announced a general strike scheduled for May 23, coinciding with the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix week...

item.title: Authorities Discover 69 Chinese Workers in Misamis Oriental Steel Plant Without Legal Work Permits
item.description: Authorities in Misamis Oriental recently uncovered a significant labor violation involving 69 Chinese nationals who were found working illegally at a steel manufacturing plant. The discovery prompted ...

item.title: Women Writers Lead 2026 International Booker Prize Shortlist Highlighting Global Fiction
item.description: The 2026 International Booker Prize shortlist presents six novels from five different languages and four continents, offering readers an extraordinary journey through diverse literary landscapes. This...

item.title: Ontario Court Upholds Peter Nygard's Sexual Assault Convictions and Prison Sentence
item.description: Ontario's highest court has rejected former fashion executive Peter Nygard's appeal, confirming his convictions for four counts of sexual assault and his 11-year prison sentence. Nygard, aged 84, was ...

item.title: Duterte's recently appointed ICC lawyer requests additional assessment of qualifications
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte has appointed a new lawyer to represent him in matters concerning the International Criminal Court (ICC). This new legal representative has formally request...

item.title: NRS recognised for reforms that boost tax compliance and national revenue
item.description: For dis time wey na economic resilience dey very important for national development, di Transparent Watch Initiative don hail di achievements of di Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) under Zacch Adedeji le...

item.title: Landlord recounts events during fatal Porepunkah police shooting
item.description: In August last year, a tragic shooting unfolded in Porepunkah when Dezi Freeman killed two police officers before fleeing into the bush, sparking Victoria’s largest manhunt. For the first time, the Fr...

item.title: Foreign faces hired to boost the image of Chinese businesses
item.description: In China, an unregulated practice exists where foreigners, often called 'white monkeys', are hired to make businesses appear more prestigious or internationally credible. This phenomenon can be traced...

item.title: Reflections on Working at British Vogue in the 1990s
item.description: Charlotte Higgins reflects on her experiences working at British Vogue and The World of Interiors during the 1990s, a period she describes as both glamorous and absurd. She recounts the unique culture...

item.title: Tanzania updates competition laws to ensure fair market and protect consumers
item.description: The Tanzanian government is actively reinforcing its competition laws to improve market efficiency, protect consumers, and attract sustainable investment. Officials say that strong competition policie...

item.title: Urgent Need for Real Policies to Improve Nigerian Workers’ Welfare
item.description: Nigerian workers have shown strong resilience despite plenty hardships like high cost of living, unstable fuel prices, and poor security across the country. This opinion piece highlights how May Day c...

item.title: Cyclists Take a Smoke Break During the 1927 Tour de France
item.description: In 1927, during the grueling Tour de France, a striking photograph captured two cyclists sharing a cigarette mid-race. Unlike today’s elite athletes who follow strict training, diet, and health regime...

item.title: Mahmud Sadis Buba Steps Down From APC House of Reps Race in Sabon Gari
item.description: Mahmud Sadis Buba, also known as Abin Al-Ajabin Zazzau, wey been dey contest for Sabon Gari Federal Constituency seat for House of Representatives don officially withdraw from the race. E post the sta...

item.title: Ena Coach Increases Fares on Nairobi-Upcountry Routes Following Fuel Price Rise
item.description: Kenyans travelling by road should brace themselves for higher travel costs as Ena Coach has raised fares on key routes across the country. The decision comes right after the Energy and Petroleum Regul...

item.title: Hamas military chief Ezzedine Al-Haddad killed in Israeli airstrike in Gaza
item.description: Hamas has confirmed that Ezzedine Al-Haddad, the head of its armed wing, was killed in an Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday. The Israeli military, along with its intelligence agency Shin Bet, claime...

item.title: Conrad Black Calls for Reviving the Commonwealth to Restore Purpose to Canada and the United Kingdom
item.description: In his latest opinion piece, Conrad Black argues that both Canada and the United Kingdom have lost their way due to prolonged mismanagement of foreign policy and domestic affairs. He criticises the UK...
